content.content:
1	"""Loads the three benchmark JSONL files into a paired N=147 instance manifest."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import hashlib
6	import json
7	import random
8	from dataclasses import dataclass
9	from pathlib import Path
10	from typing import Any, Literal
11	
12	from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.paths import (
13	    FRONTIERSCIENCE_JSONL_PATH,
14	    N_FRONTSCI_TARGET,
15	    N_SWEBENCH_TARGET,
16	    N_TAUBENCH_TARGET,
17	    SEED,
18	    SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
19	    SUBSET_SWEBENCH,
20	    SUBSET_TAUBENCH,
21	    SWEBENCH_JSONL_PATH,
22	    TAUBENCH_JSONL_PATH,
23	)
24	
25	type SubsetName = Literal["swebench", "taubench", "frontsci"]
26	
27	
28	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
29	class Instance:
30	    instance_id: str
31	    subset: str
32	    problem_text: str
33	    gold: dict[str, Any] | None
34	
35	
36	def _read_jsonl(*, path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
37	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
38	    with path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
39	        for line in f:
40	            stripped = line.strip()
41	            if len(stripped) == 0:
42	                continue
43	            obj = json.loads(stripped)
44	            assert isinstance(obj, dict), f"row in {path} is not an object: {obj!r}"
45	            rows.append(obj)
46	    return rows
47	
48	
49	def _sha256_of_file(*, path: Path) -> str:
50	    digest = hashlib.sha256()
51	    with path.open("rb") as f:
52	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(64 * 1024), b""):
53	            digest.update(chunk)
54	    return digest.hexdigest()
55	
56	
57	def _load_swebench_stratified(*, n_target: int, seed: int) -> list[Instance]:
58	    rows = _read_jsonl(path=SWEBENCH_JSONL_PATH)
59	    buckets: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}
60	    for row in rows:
61	        difficulty = str(row.get("difficulty", "unknown"))
62	        buckets.setdefault(difficulty, []).append(row)
63	    rng = random.Random(seed)
64	    for bucket_rows in buckets.values():
65	        bucket_rows.sort(key=lambda r: str(r["instance_id"]))
66	        rng.shuffle(bucket_rows)
67	    bucket_keys = sorted(buckets.keys())
68	    total_available = sum(len(buckets[k]) for k in bucket_keys)
69	    sampled: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
70	    cursor: dict[str, int] = {k: 0 for k in bucket_keys}
71	    while len(sampled) < n_target and len(sampled) < total_available:
72	        for k in bucket_keys:
73	            if len(sampled) >= n_target:
74	                break
75	            idx = cursor[k]
76	            if idx < len(buckets[k]):
77	                sampled.append(buckets[k][idx])
78	                cursor[k] = idx + 1
79	    sampled.sort(key=lambda r: str(r["instance_id"]))
80	    out: list[Instance] = []
81	    for row in sampled:
82	        instance_id = str(row["instance_id"])
83	        problem_text = str(row.get("problem_statement", ""))
84	        gold: dict[str, Any] = {
85	            "FAIL_TO_PASS": row.get("FAIL_TO_PASS"),
86	            "PASS_TO_PASS": row.get("PASS_TO_PASS"),
87	            "patch": row.get("patch"),
88	            "test_patch": row.get("test_patch"),
89	            "repo": row.get("repo"),
90	            "base_commit": row.get("base_commit"),
91	            "difficulty": row.get("difficulty"),
92	        }
93	        out.append(
94	            Instance(
95	                instance_id=instance_id,
96	                subset=SUBSET_SWEBENCH,
97	                problem_text=problem_text,
98	                gold=gold,
99	            )
100	        )
101	    return out
102	
103	
104	def _load_taubench(*, n_target: int) -> list[Instance]:
105	    rows = _read_jsonl(path=TAUBENCH_JSONL_PATH)
106	    rows.sort(key=lambda r: (str(r.get("domain", "")), int(r.get("task_index", 0))))
107	    selected = rows[:n_target]
108	    out: list[Instance] = []
109	    for row in selected:
110	        domain = str(row.get("domain", "unknown"))
111	        task_index = int(row.get("task_index", 0))
112	        instance_id = f"tau-{domain}-{task_index:04d}"
113	        problem_text = str(row.get("instruction", ""))
114	        gold: dict[str, Any] = {
115	            "outputs": row.get("outputs"),
116	            "actions": row.get("actions"),
117	            "domain": domain,
118	        }
119	        out.append(
120	            Instance(
121	                instance_id=instance_id,
122	                subset=SUBSET_TAUBENCH,
123	                problem_text=problem_text,
124	                gold=gold,
125	            )
126	        )
127	    return out
128	
129	
130	def _load_frontsci(*, n_target: int) -> list[Instance]:
131	    rows = _read_jsonl(path=FRONTIERSCIENCE_JSONL_PATH)
132	    rows.sort(key=lambda r: str(r.get("task_id", "")))
133	    selected = rows[:n_target]
134	    out: list[Instance] = []
135	    for row in selected:
136	        instance_id = str(row.get("task_id", ""))
137	        problem_text = str(row.get("problem", ""))
138	        gold: dict[str, Any] = {
139	            "solution": row.get("solution"),
140	            "domain": row.get("domain"),
141	            "difficulty": row.get("difficulty"),
142	        }
143	        out.append(
144	            Instance(
145	                instance_id=instance_id,
146	                subset=SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
147	                problem_text=problem_text,
148	                gold=gold,
149	            )
150	        )
151	    return out
152	
153	
154	def load_instances() -> list[Instance]:
155	    swe = _load_swebench_stratified(n_target=N_SWEBENCH_TARGET, seed=SEED)
156	    tau = _load_taubench(n_target=N_TAUBENCH_TARGET)
157	    fs = _load_frontsci(n_target=N_FRONTSCI_TARGET)
158	    assert len(swe) == N_SWEBENCH_TARGET, f"SWE-bench: expected {N_SWEBENCH_TARGET}, got {len(swe)}"
159	    assert len(tau) == N_TAUBENCH_TARGET, f"Tau-bench: expected {N_TAUBENCH_TARGET}, got {len(tau)}"
160	    assert len(fs) == N_FRONTSCI_TARGET, f"FrontSci: expected {N_FRONTSCI_TARGET}, got {len(fs)}"
161	    return swe + tau + fs
162	
163	
164	def sample_per_subset(*, instances: list[Instance], n_per_subset: int) -> list[Instance]:
165	    by_subset: dict[str, list[Instance]] = {}
166	    for inst in instances:
167	        by_subset.setdefault(inst.subset, []).append(inst)
168	    out: list[Instance] = []
169	    for subset_name in (SUBSET_SWEBENCH, SUBSET_TAUBENCH, SUBSET_FRONTSCI):
170	        subset_instances = by_subset.get(subset_name, [])
171	        out.extend(subset_instances[:n_per_subset])
172	    return out
173	
174	
175	def write_manifest(*, instances: list[Instance], output_path: Path) -> None:
176	    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
177	    per_subset_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
178	    per_subset_ids: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
179	    for inst in instances:
180	        per_subset_counts[inst.subset] = per_subset_counts.get(inst.subset, 0) + 1
181	        per_subset_ids.setdefault(inst.subset, []).append(inst.instance_id)
182	    manifest: dict[str, Any] = {
183	        "seed": SEED,
184	        "n_total": len(instances),
185	        "per_subset_counts": per_subset_counts,
186	        "source_sha256": {
187	            SUBSET_SWEBENCH: _sha256_of_file(path=SWEBENCH_JSONL_PATH),
188	            SUBSET_TAUBENCH: _sha256_of_file(path=TAUBENCH_JSONL_PATH),
189	            SUBSET_FRONTSCI: _sha256_of_file(path=FRONTIERSCIENCE_JSONL_PATH),
190	        },
191	        "instance_ids": [inst.instance_id for inst in instances],
192	        "per_subset_instance_ids": per_subset_ids,
193	    }
194	    output_path.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
195

1	"""Import-only smoke tests: catch ImportError, instantiate dataclasses with stub values, no IO."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import pytest
6	
7	from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code import (
8	    anthropic_shim,
9	    calibration,
10	    instance_loader,
11	    judge,
12	    main,
13	    mcnemar,
14	    metrics,
15	    paths,
16	    runner,
17	)
18	
19	
20	def test_modules_import_cleanly() -> None:
21	    assert paths.SEED == 20260502
22	    assert paths.N_TOTAL_TARGET == 147
23	    assert paths.MODEL_UNDER_TEST == "claude-sonnet-4-6"
24	    assert paths.JUDGE_MODEL_PRIMARY == "claude-sonnet-4-6"
25	    assert paths.JUDGE_MODEL_INTER == "claude-opus-4-7"
26	    assert paths.FORBIDDEN_HAIKU_MODEL_ID == "claude-haiku-4-5"
27	
28	
29	def test_cost_tracker_records_and_snapshots() -> None:
30	    tracker = anthropic_shim.CostTracker()
31	    tracker.record(model_id="claude-sonnet-4-6", prompt_tokens=1000, completion_tokens=500)
32	    snap = tracker.snapshot()
33	    assert snap["n_calls"] == 1
34	    assert snap["prompt_tokens"] == 1000
35	    assert snap["completion_tokens"] == 500
36	    expected = 1000 * 3.0 / 1_000_000.0 + 500 * 15.0 / 1_000_000.0
37	    assert abs(snap["cost_usd"] - expected) < 1e-12
38	    tracker.note_parse_failure()
39	    assert tracker.snapshot()["parse_failures"] == 1
40	
41	
42	def test_make_model_call_rejects_haiku() -> None:
43	    tracker = anthropic_shim.CostTracker()
44	    with pytest.raises(AssertionError):
45	        anthropic_shim.make_model_call(
46	            model_id=paths.FORBIDDEN_HAIKU_MODEL_ID,
47	            cost_tracker=tracker,
48	            max_tokens=64,
49	        )
50	
51	
52	def test_judge_outcome_rejects_haiku() -> None:
53	    instance = instance_loader.Instance(
54	        instance_id="stub-1",
55	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
56	        problem_text="What is 2+2?",
57	        gold={"solution": "Ground truth answer: 4"},
58	    )
59	    pred = judge.Prediction(
60	        instance_id="stub-1",
61	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
62	        variant=paths.VARIANT_A,
63	        final_answer="The answer is 4.",
64	        final_confidence=0.9,
65	        cost_usd=0.0,
66	        trajectory_path=None,
67	    )
68	    tracker = anthropic_shim.CostTracker()
69	    with pytest.raises(AssertionError):
70	        judge.judge_outcome(
71	            instance=instance,
72	            prediction=pred,
73	            cost_tracker=tracker,
74	            model_id=paths.FORBIDDEN_HAIKU_MODEL_ID,
75	        )
76	
77	
78	def test_compute_program_truth_frontsci_substring_match() -> None:
79	    instance = instance_loader.Instance(
80	        instance_id="fs-1",
81	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
82	        problem_text="What is 2+2?",
83	        gold={"solution": "Ground truth answer: 4"},
84	    )
85	    pred_pass = judge.Prediction(
86	        instance_id="fs-1",
87	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
88	        variant=paths.VARIANT_A,
89	        final_answer="After computation, the answer is 4.",
90	        final_confidence=None,
91	        cost_usd=0.0,
92	        trajectory_path=None,
93	    )
94	    pred_fail = judge.Prediction(
95	        instance_id="fs-1",
96	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
97	        variant=paths.VARIANT_A,
98	        final_answer="I think the result is seven.",
99	        final_confidence=None,
100	        cost_usd=0.0,
101	        trajectory_path=None,
102	    )
103	    assert judge.compute_program_truth(instance=instance, prediction=pred_pass) is True
104	    assert judge.compute_program_truth(instance=instance, prediction=pred_fail) is False
105	
106	
107	def test_compute_program_truth_swebench_returns_none() -> None:
108	    instance = instance_loader.Instance(
109	        instance_id="swe-1",

111	        problem_text="Fix the bug.",
112	        gold={"repo": "x/y", "FAIL_TO_PASS": "[]"},

126	def test_calibration_ece_basic() -> None:
127	    confs = [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9]
128	    outs = [False, False, True, True, True]
129	    result = calibration.compute_ece_10bin(confidences=confs, outcomes=outs)
130	    assert result.n_total == 5
131	    assert 0.0 <= result.ece <= 1.0
132	    assert len(result.bins) == 10
133

135	def test_mcnemar_exact_small_sample() -> None:
136	    result = mcnemar.mcnemar_paired(b=3, c=1)
137	    assert result.method == "exact_binomial"
138	    assert 0.0 < result.p_value <= 1.0
139	    pairwise = mcnemar.pairwise_mcnemar(
140	        success_first=[True, True, True, False, False],
141	        success_second=[False, True, True, True, True],
142	    )
143	    assert "p_value" in pairwise
144	    assert "discordant_b" in pairwise
145	    assert "discordant_c" in pairwise
146	
147	
148	def test_mcnemar_chi_squared_large_sample() -> None:
149	    result = mcnemar.mcnemar_paired(b=20, c=10)
150	    assert result.method == "chi_squared_continuity"
151	    assert 0.0 < result.p_value <= 1.0
152	
153	
154	def test_mcnemar_trivial_zero_discordant() -> None:
155	    result = mcnemar.mcnemar_paired(b=0, c=0)
156	    assert result.method == "trivial"
157	    assert result.p_value == 1.0
158

160	def test_metrics_compute_with_minimal_inputs() -> None:
161	    instance_a = instance_loader.Instance(
162	        instance_id="i1",
163	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
164	        problem_text="p",
165	        gold=None,
166	    )
167	    instance_b = instance_loader.Instance(
168	        instance_id="i2",
169	        subset=paths.SUBSET_TAUBENCH,
170	        problem_text="p",
171	        gold=None,
172	    )
173	    pred_a_v_a = judge.Prediction(
174	        instance_id="i1",
175	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
176	        variant=paths.VARIANT_A,
177	        final_answer="x",
178	        final_confidence=None,
179	        cost_usd=0.001,
180	        trajectory_path=None,
181	    )
182	    pred_a_v_b = judge.Prediction(
183	        instance_id="i1",
184	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
185	        variant=paths.VARIANT_B,
186	        final_answer="x",
187	        final_confidence=0.6,
188	        cost_usd=0.002,
189	        trajectory_path=None,
190	    )
191	    pred_a_v_c = judge.Prediction(
192	        instance_id="i1",
193	        subset=paths.SUBSET_FRONTSCI,
194	        variant=paths.VARIANT_C,
195	        final_answer="x",
196	        final_confidence=None,
197	        cost_usd=0.003,
198	        trajectory_path=None,
199	    )
200	    jr_pass = judge.JudgeResult(success=True, rationale="ok", raw_response="VERDICT: PASS")
201	    jr_fail = judge.JudgeResult(success=False, rationale="bad", raw_response="VERDICT: FAIL")
202	    judge_results: dict[str, dict[str, metrics.JudgePerInstance]] = {
203	        paths.VARIANT_A: {"i1": metrics.JudgePerInstance(sonnet=jr_pass, opus=None)},
204	        paths.VARIANT_B: {"i1": metrics.JudgePerInstance(sonnet=jr_pass, opus=jr_pass)},
205	        paths.VARIANT_C: {"i1": metrics.JudgePerInstance(sonnet=jr_fail, opus=None)},
206	    }
207	    out = metrics.compute_all_metrics(
208	        predictions_by_variant={
209	            paths.VARIANT_A: [pred_a_v_a],
210	            paths.VARIANT_B: [pred_a_v_b],
211	            paths.VARIANT_C: [pred_a_v_c],
212	        },
213	        judge_results=judge_results,
214	        manifest=[instance_a, instance_b],
215	    )
216	    assert out["success_rate_a"] == 1.0
217	    assert out["success_rate_b"] == 1.0
218	    assert out["success_rate_c"] == 0.0
219	    assert "rq5_strict_inequality_supported" in out
220	    assert out["bonferroni_alpha"] == 0.025
221	    assert out["efficiency_inference_cost_per_item_usd"] is not None
222

232	    args = parser.parse_args(["--full"])
233	    assert args.full is True
234

238	    assert hasattr(runner, "VariantRunSummary")
